_comment: This is a SAMPLE override file. Copy it, rename to {your-post-slug}.json, fill in only the fields you want to override. Empty/missing fields fall back to the RSS feed data.
title: What Sinners Got Right About the Cost of Selling Your Soul
seoTitle: Sinners (2025) Review: Coogler's Period Horror and Cultural Memory
subtitle: On Ryan Coogler's vampire myth and the price of leaving home.
metaDescription: A long-form essay on Sinners, Ryan Coogler's 1930s Mississippi vampire film — what it gets right about Black cultural memory and what makes it more than a genre exercise.
_canonicalNote: Set to 'mirror' here because we added a 400-word original intro and FAQ section that don't exist on the Substack version. If this were just a copy of the post, leave it 'substack'.
introSummary: This is the expanded version of the essay. The original Substack post focused on the film itself; here I added a longer setup on the history of Black horror as a genre, plus an FAQ at the bottom for readers who haven't seen the film yet.

faq.q: Do I need to have seen Sinners to read this essay?
faq.a: No. The first half is spoiler-light and works as a primer on Coogler's project. I flag spoilers before they happen.

faq.q: Is this a review or an analysis?
faq.a: Both. The first third functions as a review for readers deciding whether to watch. The rest is closer to cultural criticism.
